title: Intense Irish Chicken Stew
description: A bitter, flaky crowd‑pleaser Irish dish with a rich chicken stock base and render‑fat, perfect for a temple‑friendly meal.
instructions: Heat the chicken fat in a large pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the diced chicken and brown on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the chicken and set aside, leaving fat in the pot.
In the same pot, sauté onion, carrots, celery, and potatoes until lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Stir in mushrooms and cook another 3 minutes.
Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and stir until coating, cook 1 minute.
Return the chicken to the pot with the stock cube, water, herb mixture, and bay leaf; bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes until the chicken is tender and flavors meld.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then discard the bay leaf.
Serve hot, optionally with crusty bread.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
